title: IMF Executive Board approved Sri Lanka’s fifth and sixth reviews under the Extended Fund Facility, clearing roughly $695 million.
body_paragraphs: The decision increased cumulative disbursements to about $2.4 billion under the four‑year arrangement for balance‑of‑payments support (a multiyear programme that releases tranches as reforms advance). The lender’s staff projected growth of close to 3% in 2026, weaker than earlier estimates, citing higher energy costs and softer tourism after the Middle East conflict.

Price pressures were expected to average near 5% this year, with official reserves envisaged to reach around $8.6 billion by end‑2026. The review flagged the need to keep revenue measures on track, advance power sector restructuring, and tighten public financial management, while noting that performance against quantitative targets at end‑2025 remained broadly on course.

title: Central Bank lifted the Overnight Policy Rate by 100 basis points to 8.75%.
body_paragraphs: The monetary authority cited wider risks to price stability and the external account after fuel‑driven cost increases and a weaker currency. Headline inflation rose to 5.4% in April on the latest reading, reversing earlier disinflation, while private‑sector credit had begun to expand faster than expected.

Gross buffers stood near $6.8 billion at end‑April despite continued debt service. The new stance aims to cool demand and ease second‑round effects from energy tariff adjustments, with the corridor for the Standing Deposit and Lending facilities (the floor and ceiling around the policy rate) reset at 8.25% and 9.25%.

title: Attorney General told the Supreme Court that indictments would be filed within a month against the former police chief over the 2022 Galle Face attacks.
body_paragraphs: The timeline was placed on record during an appeal relating to an earlier order that had quashed a directive to list the official as a suspect. A three‑judge bench fixed the matter for further hearing on 2 September, following submissions by state counsel and the defence.

The case concerns violence at the main protest site during the ‘Aragalaya’ movement. An indictment (a formal criminal charge filed in a higher court) would render some pending proceedings unnecessary, senior defence counsel argued while reserving the right to respond once charges are served.
bold_phrases: fixed the matter for further hearing on 2 September

title: Cabinet approved the ‘Surakimu Lanka’ National Energy Security Mission to shift demand and cut import exposure.
body_paragraphs: The programme targets load management toward periods with stronger renewable output and promotes efficiency through behavioural change. Implementation will be coordinated across the Presidential Secretariat, the Energy Ministry, the Sustainable Energy Authority, and a dedicated Clean Sri Lanka unit.

Officials framed the plan as a response to hydro shortfalls, global price spikes, and supply disruptions linked to geopolitics. The framework blends public messaging, tariff‑design tools, and institutional coordination to curb peak demand and reduce reliance on thermal generation.
bold_phrases: shift demand toward periods with stronger renewable output

title: Election Commission said it can conduct Provincial Council polls once Parliament removes legal hurdles.
body_paragraphs: At a meeting of the select committee on the electoral system, officials from the justice arm outlined obstacles and potential remedies. The poll authority confirmed operational readiness under any framework enacted on the committee’s recommendation.
Lawmakers were told that pending clarifications include franchise provisions and constituency design. The chair indicated a report would go to the full House after consultations with legal advisors.
bold_phrases: select committee on the electoral system
report would go to the full House

title: Police said efforts are under way to repatriate 89 fugitives listed on Interpol Red Notices.
body_paragraphs: A spokesperson reported that eight were returned from the United Arab Emirates on 27 May after an intelligence‑led operation, following 21 arrivals earlier in the month. Since 2024, 32 wanted persons have been brought back and handed to specialist units.
A Red Notice (an international request to locate and provisionally detain for extradition) remains active for the remaining suspects linked to organised crime and narcotics.

title: State petroleum operator said fuel stocks are adequate through end‑July as price review looms.
body_paragraphs: Management confirmed arrivals of two crude cargoes on 28 and 31 May and said all shipments scheduled for the month had discharged. Officials added that a pricing decision would be taken at month‑end in light of international markets and exchange movements.

The company urged motorists to avoid panic buying and noted that allocation controls via the digital quota remain in place. A separate briefing indicated no change had been approved to tariffs at the time of the statement.
bold_phrases: pricing decision would be taken at month‑end

title: Supreme Court held that a lender may proceed directly against a guarantor where a waiver clause permits it.
body_paragraphs: Dismissing an appeal, the bench affirmed a commercial court ruling that enforced a personal undertaking after credit facilities were defaulted by the principal obligor. The judgment turned on an express term allowing action without first suing the borrower.

The court also rejected a defence that documents had been signed in blank, saying such a plea requires special proof, for example illiteracy or incapacity. The decision clarifies enforcement where guarantees contain ‘no‑sue‑first’ language (a contractual waiver of the benefit of discussion).
bold_phrases: contractual waiver of the benefit of discussion

title: Twenty‑one alleged gang figures deported from Dubai arrived under escort and were handed to investigators.
body_paragraphs: Flights were diverted to Mattala due to weather, police said, identifying two high‑profile returnees earmarked for questioning by central units. Authorities said case files cover serious offences, including racketeering and drug trafficking.
Further screenings and charge decisions will follow after statements are recorded. Additional removals are being coordinated with foreign counterparts.

title: Police warned motorists about fake traffic‑fine messages mimicking the GovPay platform.
body_paragraphs: Officers urged the public not to click links in unsolicited texts and to pay only via official gateways after a paper ticket is issued. The scam involved a bogus site designed to harvest card data by posing as the state’s e‑payment portal for penalties.

GovPay (the Government’s online settlement system for fixed penalties) requires a valid reference generated by an officer. The cyber unit said takedown requests had been filed and asked victims to lodge complaints promptly.
bold_phrases: bogus site designed to harvest card data
valid reference generated by an officer
